@lindsaystamp3 @rajatsoni UTXO management is pretty important as well.  When you buy on an exchange, don't send every transaction over to cold storage right away.  Wait until you have AT LEAST 1M sats before sending to Cold Storage, preferably more.  This consolidates all of your sats into a single transaction to cold storage, easier on the network, and easier on future transaction fees because there will be less data stored in each transaction.

Although everyone loves NGU technology, NGU and capital adoption doesn’t happen overnight.  This will take us by surprise when nobody is expecting it.   Develop your thesis. Stick to your thesis. Hold your assets. Accumulate assets.   Volatility is a feature, not a bug.   Stay solvent, breathe, enjoy your life, stack sats & microstrats

🧢 Thinking caps on for some BTC Econ🧢   (Long post Trigger warning)   There’s a world where $MSTR holds 2M BTC and a total of 4M BTC worth of assets.   Let that sink in.  How is that possible?   While there are only 21M Bitcoin, BTC will likely be the measuring stick for future assets, BUT, not all assets on the planet will be for sale at the same time.  This means there will be > 21M BTC of value in assets spread across the planet (real estate, equities, bonds, commodities, etc)  Dynamic Predator and Prey economic phase cycles can give us a glimpse into a conceptual future, which is already playing out.   Enter Coyotes (predator) vs. rabbits (prey)  As population of rabbits (prey) increases, coyote (predator) population thrives on the EASY hunting of the increased rabbits, (difficulty of hunting is reduced). As coyote population thrives, rabbit population dwindles, coyote population then suffers because it is far more difficult to hunt rabbits.  As coyote population suffers, rabbit population begins to thrive again. Cycle repeats, dynamically, in perpetuity attempting to approach equilibrium.   This dynamic economic concept is a phase cycle that occurs over time. It represents the natural & constant push / pull pressure attempting to achieve equilibrium, always approaching it, but never achieving it.   Enter BTC (predator) vs All other assets (prey).   As all other assets (prey) increase in fiat value, BTC (new predator) is invented. As BTC value increases, all other asset value drops relative to BTC, BTC Yield is easy and efficient & BTC thrives.  As BTC thrives, BTC yield becomes more difficult, BTC capital then rotates and consumes other assets at depressed relative values, until BTC yield difficulty becomes attractive again.  All are forms of “BTC yield” and value generation, if everything in the world is measured in BTC.   The difference between Coyotes & Rabbits is time scale and effort.    BTC v all other assets may be on a 20+ year phase cycle.   The point is: Value in the world stored in other assets is NOT going to completely evaporate. It will be constantly pushing and pulling towards equilibrium, forever.   Corporations adopting a BTC treasury strategy are going to have the financial strength and FLEXIBILITY in the future to consume other assets at depressed relative valuations, when measured in BTC.  This will occur when (and only when) the pure BTC yield difficulty is no longer the most efficient capital allocation.   For MOST, Pure BTC yield difficulty may be the most efficient capital allocation for the next 12 - 20 years, while we’re in the middle of the “digital gold rush”.  This equation may evolve over time when BTC is harder to mine, and more difficult to yield.   So buckle up, mine as much BTC as humanly possible in the next 12 years, sit on your hands, enjoy the ride.   *I believe this concept is why many BTC OG’s have sold some BTC positions* - it was no longer efficient in their life time and life span to increase their own personal BTC yield. Another reason insurance premiums are going 🟥👆🏼  (Nerdy reinsurance capital market 🧵 for those interested)  These large hailstone events have become much more common (larger bullseye effect)   After these types of events, predatory roof and siding contractors will knock on neighboring doors without damage to “inspect their roofs”, and miraculously find “hail damage” (aka hammer holes).   Homeowner gets a new roof  Contractor gets a job at increased demand surge pricing  Insurance company gets screwed   Some states also have rules that if the damaged siding / windows / roofing / paint color can not be matched exactly, then the insurance company is required to re-side the entire house, or replace EVERYTHING.   In large industry wide events, these costs are passed on to the reinsurance industry.   The reinsurance market then raises rates and deductibles the following year, which gets passed down to the consumers.   Regional insurance companies, that lack global or nationwide diversification generally get hit the hardest & lack the ability to spread costs across their entire portfolios.   I cut my teeth in the reinsurance industry focused on Midwest hail reinsurance treaties.  We had a client that over the course of 3 years saw their reinsurance cost quadruple, and their retention triple.   The very first layer of their reinsurance program was $7.5M in excess of $7.5M.  The insurance company paid $4.875M (65% “rate on line”) for that coverage annually.   This included three limits of loss.   - they could have three losses of $15M before the reinsurance was exhausted.  - For each limit of liability that was exhausted, the insurance company had to pay ANOTHER $4.875M in premium to “fill the coverage back up (reinstatement premium).  This reinsurance program was priced as effectively a guaranteed loss each year, and attracted some very interesting capital allocators (high stakes, gamblers).  The reinsurance company would set aside $7.5M for covering the hail event, and get paid $4.87M for locking up that capital.  If there is no event, the reinsurance company makes 65% (less brokerage fees) on the capital allocated. Vice versa if there’s a loss.   Infinite creativity in the reinsurance market.  If you can dream it & sell it, you can do it.   Really looking forward to bringing that skillset to life in the Bitcoin markets 🔥

Feeling really proud of @Strive and the Strive team today, reaching Par with SATA.  We’ve been working our butts off since our reverse merger 7 months ago (Sept 2025) and long before that.  We’ve faced continuous adversity, plenty of doubters, and relentless market headwinds, yet tackled every single obstacle in stride.  Our team is on a heater. Dialed. In the zone.  In just seven months as a public company, we’ve:  1. Acquired 13,628 BTC, making us the 10th-largest publicly traded holder of Bitcoin  2. Completed the first acquisition of a BTC Treasury Co.  3. Become the 2nd issuer of Digital Credit SATA   4. Become the first public company with perpetual preferred equity–only amplification  All in less than 7 months since our listing.  SATA, our variable rate cumulative preferred equity, is just 5 months old, and traded ~$43 million in volume today and ~$1.28 billion in volume since launch (104 trading days).  To put that into perspective:  – JP Morgan preferred (JPM‑PD) traded ~$5.9 million in volume today – There’s ~$1.5 billion of JPM‑PD outstanding (3.5x larger than SATA) – Yet it has traded ~$1.28 billion in volume over the last 515 trading days (since April 10, 2024) ___________  The team just keeps executing, no matter the challenge or market sentiment, and we’re only just getting started.  We’re a very tight knit team, and there are many people you don’t regularly see, behind the scenes, working relentlessly on our shared mission.   (You should see the number of Celcius cans & cups of coffee consumed... 😲)  That said, job’s not finished.  We’ll wake up tomorrow and be right back to building the next chapter of our story.  Major thank you to the supporters, and folks on the journey with us.    Cheers, PJ 🫡  _____________ Will leave you with a excerpt from Edgar Allen Guest:  "It Couldn't Be Done"  "There are thousands to tell you it cannot be done,       There are thousands to prophesy failure;  There are thousands to point out to you one by one,       The dangers that wait to assail you.
